hypothesis: If models are retrained on chemically accurate, Kekulized GEOM-Drugs datasets with corrected valency rules, then the generated molecules will exhibit smaller geometric deviations (Δr, Δθ, Δφ) and lower relaxation energy ΔE_relax relative to GFN2-xTB-optimized structures than models trained on the original datasets, because improved valency accuracy and reduced aromatic ambiguity yield more chemically consistent 3D conformations.
